description: The app shows the default SVG preview on first load.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the editor contains inline SVG markup
Verify the preview panel renders the default image icon

description: The React checkbox controls whether JSX output is shown.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the React checkbox is visible in the output panel
Verify JSX output is visible by default
Uncheck React and verify the JSX output section disappears
Re-check React and verify the JSX output section returns

description: A valid SVG can be converted to React-safe JSX and copied.
steps: Open the app home page
Confirm the JSX output includes React-safe attributes such as strokeWidth and className
Click Copy
Verify the copy action succeeds and the button shows copied feedback

description: Malformed SVG input shows an inline error, disables copy, and preserves the last valid preview.
steps: Open the app home page
Replace the editor contents with malformed SVG markup
Verify an inline error message appears
Verify the Copy button is disabled
Verify the preview still shows the last valid SVG render

description: The workbench stays usable on desktop and mobile without page-level overflow.
steps: Open the app at a desktop viewport such as 1280x800
Verify the editor and output panels appear side-by-side without page scrolling
Open the app at a mobile viewport such as 390x844
Verify the editor and output panels stack vertically without page scrolling

description: The project metadata and harness docs use the hyphenated name `svg-viewer`.
steps: Inspect the root package metadata
Verify the package name is `svg-viewer` in both `package.json` and `package-lock.json`
Verify the checked-in harness docs identify the repo as `svg-viewer`
Confirm the checked-in package metadata and harness docs no longer use the old `svgviewer` name

description: The workbench uses one shared output viewport with Preview, React, and PNG tabs while the editor highlights SVG syntax and keeps the last valid output visible.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the output panel shows Preview, React, and PNG tabs above one shared viewport
Verify Preview renders the default SVG, React shows JSX output, and PNG shows a raster preview without changing panels
Verify the source editor visually distinguishes tags, attribute names, and attribute values while still accepting typed input
Replace the editor contents with malformed SVG markup and verify an inline error appears while the last valid Preview and PNG remain visible

description: The output panel keeps Preview, React, and PNG as permanent tabs and adds a shared size slider that enlarges Preview and PNG without overflowing the viewport.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the output header no longer shows the old Live chip or React checkbox
Verify Preview, React, and PNG tabs are always visible
Verify a shared size slider is visible in the output toolbar
Verify the default Preview render is much larger than the intrinsic 24x24 icon
Adjust the size slider and verify Preview and PNG resize together without overflowing their stage
Verify the React tab keeps copy-ready JSX output and does not resize its code text when the slider changes

description: The workbench removes non-essential helper copy from the header, editor footer, and output panel while preserving actionable warning and error states.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the header no longer shows the long explanatory paragraph under the title
Verify the editor footer no longer shows the syntax-highlighting helper copy or the neutral sanitization note
Verify the output panel no longer shows a default neutral helper notice under the viewport
Replace the editor contents with malformed SVG markup and verify the inline error state still appears

description: The Source and Output panels use a shared outer grid treatment while the inner code/image viewports stay clear, and the output controls become full-width rows above and below the viewport.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the Source and Output panel bodies share the same outer grid treatment
Verify the actual code viewport is solid with no grid behind the syntax-highlighted text
Verify the actual Preview and PNG viewport is solid with no grid behind the rendered art
Verify the Preview, React, and PNG controls appear in a full-width row above the viewport
Verify the Size slider appears in a full-width row below the viewport for Preview and PNG
Verify the Size slider is visually hidden on React while its row height remains reserved
Verify malformed SVG still shows the inline error while the last valid output remains visible

description: The workbench moves Copy and Download below the viewport, neutralizes the code and preview work areas, preserves the user's valid source structure in JSX output, and gives the Output panel more height on mobile.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the top control row contains only Preview, React, and PNG tabs
Verify the Size row appears below the viewport and Copy or Download appears in a separate action row below that
Verify the Preview action row stays reserved but empty
Verify the actual code and image work areas use neutral gray or white surfaces with no blue tint
Verify the React tab shows JSX-only output that preserves the user's line breaks and attribute order while converting React-safe attributes
Verify malformed SVG still shows the inline error while the last valid output remains visible
Verify the mobile layout at 390x844 gives the Output panel enough height to display the image without clipping into the bottom rows

description: The output tabs are labeled SVG, PNG, and React in that order.
steps: Open the app home page
Verify the top output tabs read SVG, PNG, and React from left to right
Verify the first tab shows the live SVG render
Verify the middle tab shows the PNG preview
Verify the right tab shows the React JSX output

description: The workbench stacks earlier, allows page scrolling on medium and mobile screens, keeps Source compact, and gives Output more vertical space without clipping the viewport or controls.
steps: Open the app at 1365x900 and verify the workbench stays side-by-side without page scrolling
Open the app at 992x1131 and 768x1024 and verify the layout stacks vertically with page scrolling enabled
Verify the Output panel is visibly taller than Source on stacked layouts and that the viewport is not clipped by the size or action rows
Open the app at 390x844 and verify the SVG/PNG viewport remains clearly visible above the bottom controls
Verify the SVG, PNG, and React tabs still work and that Copy and Download PNG remain aligned under the viewport

description: The output workflow uses calm, precise motion for tab switching, viewport swaps, slider visibility, and copy feedback while respecting reduced-motion preferences.
steps: Open the app home page and switch between SVG, PNG, and React
Verify the active tab highlight moves cleanly and the viewport uses a short fade and slight translate transition
Verify the Size row still hides on React with a subtle reserved-space transition
Click Copy on the React tab and verify the Copied state uses a short success transition
Verify Download PNG keeps simple hover and press feedback without fake success animation
Verify reduced-motion mode disables decorative workflow motion

description: Medium desktop widths use the same two-column, full-height shell as large screens instead of stacking Source and Output vertically.
steps: Open the app at a medium desktop viewport such as 992x1131
Verify Source and Output render side by side in two columns
Verify the page does not scroll vertically and the shell uses the full viewport height
Verify the output viewport, size row, and action row all remain visible without clipping
Verify the mobile layout still remains stacked below the desktop breakpoint

description: The live app shows external icon resource links in a compact footer bar, and screens below 960px use a fixed no-scroll stacked layout where Source and Output split the workspace 50/50.
steps: Open the app at 1365x900 and verify the app remains two-column without page scrolling
Verify the live UI shows footer links for Lucide Icons, Iconoir, Phosphor Icons, and Heroicons with an icon before each label
Open the app at 900x1000 and verify Source and Output stack vertically while each occupies half the available workspace height
Verify the page does not scroll at 900x1000 and that Source and Output scroll internally when needed
Open the app at 390x844 and verify the same fixed stacked 50/50 layout with a compact horizontally usable footer
Verify SVG, PNG, and React tabs still work and malformed SVG still preserves the last valid output

description: Interactive workbench controls use color and highlight feedback on hover without visually hopping upward.
steps: Open the app home page
Hover the SVG, PNG, and React tabs and verify they do not move upward
Hover the Copy or Download action button and verify it does not move upward
Hover the footer resource chips and verify they do not move upward
Verify the active tab still feels selected via color and highlight rather than lift
Click or tap the same controls and verify the small pressed-state feedback still works

description: The React tab outputs a full copy-ready React component wrapper instead of raw JSX, and the Copied feedback clears faster.
steps: Open the app home page and switch to the React tab
Verify the output begins with `import * as React from "react";` and includes `const SVGComponent = (props) => (`
Verify the root `<svg>` includes `{...props}` and the output ends with `export default SVGComponent;`
Paste a different valid SVG and verify the React output updates into the same component form with React-safe attributes
Click Copy and verify the button changes to Copied, then returns to Copy noticeably faster than before

description: On stacked small-screen layouts, the output viewport, size slider, and action row stay in a clear vertical stack with no overlap into the preview area.
steps: Open the app at 390x844 and verify the output viewport is fully visible with the Size slider below it and no overlap into the rendered SVG
Open the app at 700x900 and 900x1000 and verify the same clear output stack on the small-screen layout
Verify the page still avoids page-level scrolling on these stacked layouts
Paste malformed SVG markup and verify the inline error still appears while the last valid output remains visible

description: The Next.js dev server explicitly allows the repo's 127.0.0.1 loopback origin so internal `/_next/*` requests no longer emit the allowedDevOrigins warning.
steps: Start the app with `bash ./init.sh`
Request a `/_next/*` dev resource with the Origin header set to `http://127.0.0.1:<port>`
Verify the request succeeds without logging the cross-origin allowedDevOrigins warning
Open the app in a browser and verify the default SVG preview still renders
